title: erophila verna subsp. boerhaavii
intro: Erophila verna subsp. boerhaavii, also known as spring whitlow-grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of spring whitlow-grass include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
